expectations.text: Asks pointed questions before writing prose, grounding the section in specifics
expectations.evidence: Asks 3 specific questions: (1) what a typical PRD cycle looked like in calendar time, (2) when you say 'theater' — theater for whom?, (3) what was your specific role and what did it feel like? Each pulls context from the lesson.

expectations.text: Pulls specific details from other sections of the lesson file to inform the draft
expectations.evidence: Pulls the merchant pinning example, '50% of the time we got it wrong', '4-10 weeks', the 13 Golden Conversations reference, and COO feedback patterns from the Core Insight section.

expectations.text: Generates suggested prose for the target section
expectations.evidence: Full 'Suggested Prose' section with 4 paragraphs of draft prose for 'The Problem This Solves'.

expectations.text: Flags sentences or claims the author should verify — does not assume all inferences are correct
expectations.evidence: 4 explicit flags in 'Readback & Flags': questions whether 'document formatted for a process' is the right framing, notes the merchant pinning example might lose impact if used twice, asks if '50%' is real or estimated, flags the last sentence as too generic.

expectations.text: Prose sounds like the author (direct, first-person, honest) not generic AI writing
expectations.evidence: Prose uses first person, direct sentences ('Here's how product development worked before'), and matches the lesson's tone. No hedging language or filler.

expectations.text: Provides a readback or verification section checking whether prose matches author's intent
expectations.evidence: 'Readback & Flags' section with 4 items, plus a 'Does this sound like you?' closing that identifies the section still needs a distinctly personal anchoring detail.

expectations.text: Stays focused on the requested section, does not draft the entire lesson
expectations.evidence: Entire output focuses on 'The Problem This Solves' section. References other sections only to inform the draft, not to rewrite them.
